ladder: 1. Apply heat to a single protein in a pan (sear steak, cook chicken).
2. Fat as a medium + basting; rest and doneness by feel/temp.
3. Pan sauce off the fond (deglaze, mount with butter).
5. Saute aromatics + vegetables to the right point.
6. Cook a starch in the pot (rice, pasta, potatoes) to correct texture.
7. Time a full plate: protein + veg + starch finishing together.

constraints.equipment: one pan + one pot, stovetop only, no oven

history.dish: Chicken Thighs with Lemon-Butter Pan Sauce
history.note: First solo pan sauce — deglazed the fond, mounted cold butter, lemon as the acid. Cleared rung 3.

history.dish: Bacon, Eggs & Rice with Homemade Chili Oil
history.note: Improvised a chili oil from bacon fat, minced garlic, spicy chili-infused olive oil, and chili flakes over bacon, eggs, and rice. Liked it but too spicy — used too much spicy oil. Lesson: heat compounds; build from mild and add the spicy oil by the teaspoon off heat, tasting up.

options: Chicken with Creamy Mushroom Pan Sauce
